prompt: Walk me through the open change orders on Westfield and the cost exposure.

failures: expected at least one of [getChangeOrderDetails, queryChangeOrders] to fire — none did
score.finalText:
Current read: Active closeout/commercial coordination with LOI/warranty language, past-due invoice follow-up, change-order signatures, and remaining work signals.
What changed recently: Westfield Collective has active late-stage communication around LOI/warranty language, change-order signatures, remaining work, and payment follow-up.
Financial/change-management exposure: The packet flags pending cost exposure and payment pressure: past-due invoice communication is present, and older plumbing/change-order scope still needs formal cost confirmation. Change-management risk centers on plumbing scope, floor-sink changes, mop-sink/Pepsi-line additions, and unsigned change-order documentation.
Schedule/operational risk: Operational risk is tied to closeout readiness: remaining items, patio/grade decisions, health-inspection plumbing fixes, and vendor coordination have appeared in recent project communications.
Decisions/open questions/follow-ups: Open decisions include whether warranty/governing-law language is acceptable and whether change-order signatures/documentation are complete. Likely missed follow-ups are the LeMaster/warranty wording response, past-due invoice resolution, change-order signatures, and confirming remaining work ownership.
Recommended next action:
- Verify latest post-April-22 communications before making client-facing claims.
- Check structured budget, commitments, direct costs, and change orders before quoting exposure dollars.
- Assign owners/dates for warranty language, change-order signatures, past-due invoices, and remaining items.
Evidence basis and confidence: The packet links to real project communications, but formal cost/status values still require structured tools. 1223 project document/source rows, 544 project memories, 0 project_emails rows. Latest packet source date: 2026-04-22T00:00:00.000Z. Gaps: project_emails has zero project-scoped rows for project 43; manual packet does not replace structured financial verification; post-packet communications must be checked for stale answers. [Source: Teams DM: Westfield Collective] [Source: Email: TAG 04/21/26 Westfield Collective - Past Due Invoices] [Source: Westfield Collective: Change Order Discussion]


Packet status: working sample. Use this as the prepared baseline, not as proof that nothing changed after 2026-04-22T00:00:00+00:00.
Resolved target: Westfield Collective (project 43).

input.query: Walk me through the open change orders on Westfield and the cost exposure.

memories.content: The user prefers detailed, specific updates on project status, risks, and next steps for Vermillion Rise Warehouse.
User prefers high-leverage, action-oriented tasks prioritized daily for Vermillion Rise Warehouse project.
User prefers to have tasks and RFIs associated with specific project names, due dates, and assignees documented clearly.
Verify latest post-April-22 communications and check structured budget, commitments, direct costs, and change orders before quoting exposure dollars for Westfield Collective.
Westfield Collective is engaged in active closeout and commercial coordination with ongoing discussions about LOI/warranty language, change-order signatures, and remaining work.
Current project concerns include pending cost exposure, payment pressure due to past-due invoices, and unsigned change-order documentation.
The project manager and cost lead must review and clarify pending change orders and change events today to manage exposure.

reasons: compiled intelligence packet with multiple evidence cards

provider_decision.reason: Streaming model tools enabled. Provider matrix 2026-05-01 confirmed AI SDK Gateway and direct OpenAI both pass generateText + streamText with tool calls (finishReason=stop, 1 tool call, non-empty text). Disable by setting AI_ASSISTANT_DISABLE_STREAMING_MODEL_TOOLS=true.
